items.headline: Seeds of Peace Monument artwork agreement approved for Guadalupe River Park
items.summary: City Council approved an agreement with The Diane Brandenburg Survivor's Trust for the Seeds of Peace Monument, an artwork by artist Mario Chiodo to be located at Guadalupe River Park Arena Green West. The monument memorializes a significant historical commitment to peace.

items.headline: Council updates District Office budget policy for transition years
items.summary: City Council considered proposed changes to Policy 1-18, Section 20, which governs how the Office of the Mayor and City Council District Office budgets are handled during transition years. This affects how council members' office funding works between election cycles.

items.headline: Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District budget adopted for 2026-27
items.summary: City Council took action on the Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District's 2026-2027 budget and annual assessment. This district covers a commercial area on the east side of San José.

items.headline: Cupertino updates Health and Safety element of General Plan
items.summary: City Council reviewed updates to the Health and Safety Element of the Cupertino General Plan covering 2015–2040. This element addresses how the city plans to protect residents' health, safety, and quality of life over the next 15 years.

items.headline: City Council appoints permanent City Manager
items.summary: The City Council adopted a resolution appointing a permanent City Manager. This follows the staff contact Christian M. Curtis and addresses a key leadership position for the city.

items.headline: New duplex permitted on Old Piedmont Road
items.summary: A new two-story duplex with 2 units received a building permit at 2174 Old Piedmont Rd, representing a notable housing addition in a city facing significant supply constraints. At over $1 million, this is the largest residential project among recent permits issued.

items.headline: City presents draft Urban Water Master Plan and shortage response
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission reviewed Sunnyvale's Draft 2025 Urban Water Master Plan and Water Shortage Contingency Plan on May 18. These documents outline the city's water supply strategy and emergency measures for drought conditions.

items.headline: Spring community grants and neighborhood events funding opens
items.summary: City Council considered 2026 Community Events and Neighborhood Grant Program applications for the spring cycle on May 18. This annual program provides funding for local events and neighborhood initiatives.
